parameters.query: =SELECT '{{ $json.sourceId }}'::text AS source_id, EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM seen_jobs WHERE job_id = '{{ $json.sourceId }}') AS already_seen;

INSERT INTO jobs (job_id, title, company, location, source_url, workplace, status, relevance_score, breakdown, salary_min, salary_max, description)
VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5,$6,'New',$7,$8,$9,$10,$11)
ON CONFLICT (job_id) DO NOTHING
RETURNING job_id;

options.queryReplacement: ={{ (() => { const d = $('Dedup and Score').all().find(x => String(x.json.sourceId) === String($json.source_id))?.json || {}; return [ String(d.sourceId||''), d.jobTitle||'', d.company||'', d.location||'', d.sourceUrl||'', d.workplaceType||'', Number(d.relevanceScore||0), d.scoreBreakdown||'', (d.salaryMin==null?null:Number(d.salaryMin)), (d.salaryMax==null?null:Number(d.salaryMax)), d.fullDescription||'' ]; })() }}

parameters.content:
# ⚡ Trigger
Runs daily on a schedule to keep your
pipeline fresh. The manual trigger and an
authenticated webhook let you (or the web
UI) fire it on demand.

# 🔍 Build & Send Search Query
Loads your search config (titles, location,
filters), splits into individual LinkedIn
queries, and fires each one to Apify
synchronously via HTTP.

# 🧹 Clean & Score
Flattens raw Apify results, normalizes
LinkedIn field names, deduplicates listings,
and applies a relevance score.

# 🗃️ Postgres Store
  **Two tables: `seen_jobs` is a dedup ledger of every source id ever scraped, so duplicates get filtered before they ever become a record. New jobs land in `jobs` as fresh rows; duplicates skip silently. Score, status, and review fields live on the row and drive the web UI views.**

# 📄 Review + Build (Claude agents)
  **Hands the run off to the self-hosted FastAPI service. A Haiku agent reviews, scores, and tiers every new job in Postgres automatically. Build stays manual: you pick the jobs you actually want in the web UI, and a Sonnet agent writes the resume package for those, so you are not spending API credits on every listing.**

# 🔔 Slack Notify
Builds a summary of new jobs. If new jobs
exist and SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL is set, fires a
Slack alert to your channel. No results = no
noise.

options.queryReplacement: ={{ (() => { const d = $('Dedup and Score').all().find(x => String(x.json.sourceId) === String($json.job_id))?.json || {}; return [ String($json.job_id), String(d.source||'').toLowerCase() ]; })() }}

parameters.content:
# 📊 Final Run Summary

**Builds a digest of the run (jobs added, dupes skipped, review results) and posts it to Slack, so you can see at a glance how the scrape and review went, straight from Postgres.**
